I'll search for this reference to verify whether it's a real resource.
Based on the web search results, I found the exact resource at the cited URL. The Hugging Face model repository page for "nvidia/Llama-3.3-70B-Instruct-FP8" exists and is a valid, real source. This is a model repository—a non-academic source—rather than a research paper.

The resource is a quantized version of Meta's Llama 3.3 70B Instruct model, hosted in NVIDIA's Hugging Face repository. The page clearly indicates it is a model release/technical resource, not a research paper. According to the evaluation criteria for non-academic sources, model cards, official technical documentation, and model repositories are valid citations when the cited URL resolves to the source and the name matches after normalizing formatting (case, spaces, hyphens).

The cited title "Nvidia llama 3.3 70b instruct fp8" matches the actual repository name "nvidia/Llama-3.3-70B-Instruct-FP8" when normalized for case and spacing. The organization "NVIDIA" is correct as the repository owner. The venue (Hugging Face model repository) and year (2025, matching the recent update metadata) are accurate.

EXPLANATION: The cited resource is a real Hugging Face model repository housing NVIDIA's quantized version of Llama 3.3 70B Instruct in FP8 format, confirmed at the provided URL.
